The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She is said to be a child of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area08/04.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tsunaga Shrine つながじんじゃ>> - 125 Shrines|publisher=Jingu Office|accessdate=2013-10-01}} == Niikawa Shrine == Niikawa Shrine is Ranked 6th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.13 The enshrined deity is Niikawa-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She was the guardian deity of a nearby dock. Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[en:Tsunaga Shrine]]

{{Shrine | name = Tsunaga Shrine, Niikawa Shrine, Iwai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsunaga, Niikawa & Iwai shrine.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho, Kashiwazaki 153-1 | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 27 | LatitudeSec = 35.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 43 | LongitudeSec = 17.5 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Tsunaga Shrine: Sunaga-hime no Mikoto
Niikawa Shrine: Niikawa-hime no Mikoto
Iwai Shrine: Takaminakami no Mikoto | Rank = Tsunaga Shrine: Shikinaisha (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} sessha
Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine: Kōtai Jingū massha | consecration_year = Emperor Suinin, 26th year of reign | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alias = Tsunaga Shrine: Tsunaga Ōmizujinja | Fuda-sho = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Tsunaga Shrine''' (つながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|擬社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|ja|內宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|qq=}}, part of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. Located on the west bank of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], the shrine is situated at the foot of Mount Tsuzukayama, directly across from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54 This article also covers {{ill|Niikawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, both massha of the Inner Shrine and located at the same site. All three shrines were designated by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], with water deities as their enshrined deities.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == Overview == The shrines are located approximately 200 meters from the Uji Bridge, which marks the entrance to the Inner Shrine’s sacred precincts, in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.111 Within 20 meters of Tsunaga Shrine is the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|飼土橋姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 50 meters away are the {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizujinja|ja|大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizujinja|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kawai Shrine|ja|川相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|ja|熊溓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, making a total of seven shrines in the area.Ise Culture Institute (2008): pp.111, 116 The site also houses the predecessor to the Jingu Library, the old Hayasaki Library.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.117 The three shrines share a single building constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], with wooden planks for roofing. The structure faces southwest and is surrounded by a single fence of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉柵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 There is no offertory box at the site. The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She is said to be a child of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area08/04.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tsunaga Shrine つながじんじゃ>> - 125 Shrines|publisher=Jingu Office|accessdate=2013-10-01}} == Niikawa Shrine == Niikawa Shrine is Ranked 6th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.13 The enshrined deity is Niikawa-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She was the guardian deity of a nearby dock. Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[en:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

{{Shrine | name = Tsunaga Shrine, Niikawa Shrine, Iwai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsunaga, Niikawa & Iwai shrine.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho, Kashiwazaki 153-1 | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 27 | LatitudeSec = 35.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 43 | LongitudeSec = 17.5 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Tsunaga Shrine: Sunaga-hime no Mikoto
Niikawa Shrine: Niikawa-hime no Mikoto
Iwai Shrine: Takaminakami no Mikoto | Rank = Tsunaga Shrine: Shikinaisha (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} sessha
Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine: Kōtai Jingū massha | consecration_year = Emperor Suinin, 26th year of reign | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alias = Tsunaga Shrine: Tsunaga Ōmizujinja | Fuda-sho = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}} }} '''Tsunaga Shrine''' (つながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|擬社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|ja|內宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|qq=}}, part of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. Located on the west bank of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], the shrine is situated at the foot of Mount Tsuzukayama, directly across from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54 This article also covers {{ill|Niikawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, both massha of the Inner Shrine and located at the same site. All three shrines were designated by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], with water deities as their enshrined deities.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == Overview == The shrines are located approximately 200 meters from the Uji Bridge, which marks the entrance to the Inner Shrine’s sacred precincts, in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.111 Within 20 meters of Tsunaga Shrine is the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|飼土橋姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, and 50 meters away are the {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizujinja|ja|大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizujinja|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kawai Shrine|ja|川相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|ja|熊溓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumabuchi Shrine|qq=}}, making a total of seven shrines in the area.Ise Culture Institute (2008): pp.111, 116 The site also houses the predecessor to the Jingu Library, the old Hayasaki Library.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.117 The three shrines share a single building constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], with wooden planks for roofing. The structure faces southwest and is surrounded by a single fence of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉柵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 There is no offertory box at the site. The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Sessha and Massha]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Tsunaga Shrine, Niikawa Shrine, Iwai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsunaga, Niikawa & Iwai shrine.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho, Kashiwazaki 153-1 | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 27 | LatitudeSec = 35.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 43 | LongitudeSec = 17.5 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Tsunaga Shrine: Sunaga-hime no Mikoto
Niikawa Shrine: Niikawa-hime no Mikoto
Iwai Shrine: Takaminakami no Mikoto | Rank = Tsunaga Shrine: Shikinaisha (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} sessha
Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine: Kōtai Jingū massha | consecration_year = Emperor Suinin, 26th year of reign | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alias = Tsunaga Shrine: Tsunaga Ōmizujinja | Fuda-sho = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Tsunaga Shrine''' (つながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|擬社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|ja|內宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. Located on the west bank of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], the shrine is situated at the foot of Mount Tsuzukayama, directly across from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54 This article also covers {{ill|Niikawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, both massha of the Inner Shrine and located at the same site. All three shrines were designated by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], with water deities as their enshrined deities.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == Overview == The shrines are located approximately 200 meters from the Uji Bridge, which marks the entrance to the Inner Shrine’s sacred precincts, in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.111 Within 20 meters of Tsunaga Shrine is the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|飼土橋姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and 50 meters away are the {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizujinja|ja|大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizujinja|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kawai Shrine|ja|川相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水神社}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|ja|熊溓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumabuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making a total of seven shrines in the area.Ise Culture Institute (2008): pp.111, 116 The site also houses the predecessor to the Jingu Library, the old Hayasaki Library.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.117 The three shrines share a single building constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], with wooden planks for roofing. The structure faces southwest and is surrounded by a single fence of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉柵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 There is no offertory box at the site. The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She is said to be a child of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area08/04.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tsunaga Shrine つながじんじゃ>> - 125 Shrines|publisher=Jingu Office|accessdate=2013-10-01}} == Niikawa Shrine == Niikawa Shrine is Ranked 6th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.13 The enshrined deity is Niikawa-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She was the guardian deity of a nearby dock. Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17215982]] Wikidata name: Tsunaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Tsunaga Shrine, Niikawa Shrine, Iwai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsunaga, Niikawa & Iwai shrine.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho, Kashiwazaki 153-1 | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 27 | LatitudeSec = 35.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 43 | LongitudeSec = 17.5 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Tsunaga Shrine: Sunaga-hime no Mikoto
Niikawa Shrine: Niikawa-hime no Mikoto
Iwai Shrine: Takaminakami no Mikoto | Rank = Tsunaga Shrine: Shikinaisha (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} sessha
Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine: Kōtai Jingū massha | consecration_year = Emperor Suinin, 26th year of reign | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alias = Tsunaga Shrine: Tsunaga Ōmizujinja | Fuda-sho = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Tsunaga Shrine''' (つながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|擬社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|ja|內宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. Located on the west bank of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], the shrine is situated at the foot of Mount Tsuzukayama, directly across from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54 This article also covers {{ill|Niikawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, both massha of the Inner Shrine and located at the same site. All three shrines were designated by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], with water deities as their enshrined deities.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == Overview == The shrines are located approximately 200 meters from the Uji Bridge, which marks the entrance to the Inner Shrine’s sacred precincts, in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.111 Within 20 meters of Tsunaga Shrine is the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|飼土橋姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and 50 meters away are the {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizujinja|ja|大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizujinja|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kawai Shrine|ja|川相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水神社}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|ja|熊溓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumabuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making a total of seven shrines in the area.Ise Culture Institute (2008): pp.111, 116 The site also houses the predecessor to the Jingu Library, the old Hayasaki Library.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.117 The three shrines share a single building constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], with wooden planks for roofing. The structure faces southwest and is surrounded by a single fence of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉柵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 There is no offertory box at the site. The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She is said to be a child of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area08/04.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tsunaga Shrine つながじんじゃ>> - 125 Shrines|publisher=Jingu Office|accessdate=2013-10-01}} == Niikawa Shrine == Niikawa Shrine is Ranked 6th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.13 The enshrined deity is Niikawa-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She was the guardian deity of a nearby dock. Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17215982]] Wikidata name: Tsunaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsunaga Shrine, Niikawa Shrine, Iwai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsunaga, Niikawa & Iwai shrine.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho, Kashiwazaki 153-1 | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 27 | LatitudeSec = 35.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 43 | LongitudeSec = 17.5 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Tsunaga Shrine: Sunaga-hime no Mikoto
Niikawa Shrine: Niikawa-hime no Mikoto
Iwai Shrine: Takaminakami no Mikoto | Rank = Tsunaga Shrine: Shikinaisha (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} sessha
Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine: Kōtai Jingū massha | consecration_year = Emperor Suinin, 26th year of reign | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alias = Tsunaga Shrine: Tsunaga Ōmizujinja | Fuda-sho = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Tsunaga Shrine''' (つながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|擬社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|ja|內宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. Located on the west bank of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], the shrine is situated at the foot of Mount Tsuzukayama, directly across from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54 This article also covers {{ill|Niikawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Shrine|qq=|1=Shinkawa Shrine (Ise)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, both massha of the Inner Shrine and located at the same site. All three shrines were designated by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], with water deities as their enshrined deities.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == Overview == The shrines are located approximately 200 meters from the Uji Bridge, which marks the entrance to the Inner Shrine’s sacred precincts, in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.111 Within 20 meters of Tsunaga Shrine is the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|飼土橋姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and 50 meters away are the {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizujinja|ja|大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizujinja|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kawai Shrine|ja|川相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水神社}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|ja|熊溓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumabuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making a total of seven shrines in the area.Ise Culture Institute (2008): pp.111, 116 The site also houses the predecessor to the Jingu Library, the old Hayasaki Library.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.117 The three shrines share a single building constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], with wooden planks for roofing. The structure faces southwest and is surrounded by a single fence of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉柵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 There is no offertory box at the site. The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She is said to be a child of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area08/04.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tsunaga Shrine つながじんじゃ>> - 125 Shrines|publisher=Jingu Office|accessdate=2013-10-01}} == Niikawa Shrine == Niikawa Shrine is Ranked 6th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.13 The enshrined deity is Niikawa-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She was the guardian deity of a nearby dock. Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17215982]] Wikidata name: Tsunaga Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsunaga Shrine, Niikawa Shrine, Iwai Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsunaga, Niikawa & Iwai shrine.jpg|270px]] | location = [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho, Kashiwazaki 153-1 | LatitudeDeg = 34 | LatitudeMin = 27 | LatitudeSec = 35.6 | LongitudeDeg = 136 | LongitudeMin = 43 | LongitudeSec = 17.5 | Map = Japan Mie | Deities = Tsunaga Shrine: Sunaga-hime no Mikoto
Niikawa Shrine: Niikawa-hime no Mikoto
Iwai Shrine: Takaminakami no Mikoto | Rank = Tsunaga Shrine: Shikinaisha (minor), {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} sessha
Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine: Kōtai Jingū massha | consecration_year = Emperor Suinin, 26th year of reign | Main Hall = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] | Alias = Tsunaga Shrine: Tsunaga Ōmizujinja | Fuda-sho = | festival = | Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Tsunaga Shrine''' (つながじんじゃ) is a {{ill|sessha|ja|擬社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sessha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|ja|內宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū (Inner Shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, part of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]]. Located on the west bank of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], the shrine is situated at the foot of Mount Tsuzukayama, directly across from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54 This article also covers {{ill|Niikawa Shrine|ja|新川神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niikawa Shrine|qq=|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11502105}} and {{ill|Ishii Shrine|ja|石井神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine|qq=}}, both massha of the Inner Shrine and located at the same site. All three shrines were designated by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]], with water deities as their enshrined deities.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == Overview == The shrines are located approximately 200 meters from the Uji Bridge, which marks the entrance to the Inner Shrine’s sacred precincts, in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], Uji Imazai-cho.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.111 Within 20 meters of Tsunaga Shrine is the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|飼土橋姫神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and 50 meters away are the {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizujinja|ja|大水神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizujinja|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kawai Shrine|ja|川相神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大水神社}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Ōmizu Shrine|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|ja|熊溓神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumabuchi Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making a total of seven shrines in the area.Ise Culture Institute (2008): pp.111, 116 The site also houses the predecessor to the Jingu Library, the old Hayasaki Library.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.117 The three shrines share a single building constructed in [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]], with wooden planks for roofing. The structure faces southwest and is surrounded by a single fence of {{ill|tamagaki|ja|玉柵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tamagaki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 There is no offertory box at the site. The total area of the shrine grounds is approximately 1,256 square meters (379.94 tsubo).Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.251 Records from 1933 mention a wisteria tree previously located at the shrine’s entrance, believed to bestow strength to children, but the tree was already gone by that time.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.249 == Tsunaga Shrine == Tsunaga Shrine is Ranked 14th among the 27 sessha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): pp.9-10 The area was historically called "Tsunagahara" and served as a dock for boats navigating the Isuzu River. It is said that Yamato-hime no Mikoto also landed here. The shrine is referred to as "Tsunaga Ōmizujinja" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.246 The enshrined deity is Sunaga-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She is said to be a child of {{ill|Ōminakami no Mikoto|ja|大水上命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōminakami no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.{{Cite web|url=http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/shrine/area08/04.html?iframe=true&width=1000&height=700|title=Tsunaga Shrine つながじんじゃ>> - 125 Shrines|publisher=Jingu Office|accessdate=2013-10-01}} == Niikawa Shrine == Niikawa Shrine is Ranked 6th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p.13 The enshrined deity is Niikawa-hime no Mikoto, a water deity. She was the guardian deity of a nearby dock. Like the other two shrines, she is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. == Iwai Shrine == Iwai Shrine is Ranked 7th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine. The enshrined deity is Takaminakami no Mikoto, the guardian deity of stone springs. Like the other two shrines, he is said to be a child of Ōminakami no Mikoto. He is also worshipped at the {{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|ja|宇治之奴鬼神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another massha of the Inner Shrine.Ise Culture Institute (2008): p.116 == History == Tsunaga Shrine is believed to have been founded in the 26th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], according to the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki''. The ''Kōtai Jingū Ritual Record'' indicates that the shrine grounds once covered three chō (approximately 3 hectares), significantly larger than the present site. Originally situated closer to the Isuzu River, the shrine played a more prominent role in ancient rituals. After the medieval period, rituals ceased but were revived on {{ill|December 21|ja|十二月二十一日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 21|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kanbun 3 (1664), under the guidance of Ōnakatomi no Seichō, who followed the description in the ''Yamato-hime no Mikoto Seiki'' to identify the site.Shikinaisha Research Group (1990): p.247 In 1871, Niikawa Shrine and Iwai Shrine were relocated to the same grounds as Tsunaga Shrine due to interruptions in their rituals. The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:qq]]

The shrine building was reconstructed in 1916 and 1956, with major renovations carried out in 1977. == Festivals == The shrine hosts regular festivals, including the [[:en:Kinen-sai|Kinensai]] (February), {{ill|Tsukinamisai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinamisai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} (June and December), {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (October), and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niinamesai|ja|新周祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (November). Ceremonies for the New Year (January 1), Kigensetsu (February 11), and other events are performed remotely. == Flora == A 1977 survey of the grounds found 95 trees, including Kaya, Maki, Sugi, Kashi, Shii, Yabutsubaki, Inubiwa, and Akamegashiwa. Notable specimens include four camphor trees over 80 cm in diameter, with the largest measuring over 150 cm. == Access == ; To Uji Bridge * From [[:en:Japan Railways Group|JR]] [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangu Line]] or [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Kintetsu Yamada Line]], alight at [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] and take a Mie Kotsu Bus to "Naiku-mae," approximately 15 minutes.Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation * By car, 5 minutes south from either the Ise West IC or Ise IC. ; From Uji Bridge * Cross the Uji Bridge rotary and walk approximately 200 meters (4 minutes). == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0101.html Tsunaga Shrine (with Niikawa and Iwai Shrines)] - Jingu Worshipers' Association {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|津長神社|version=98170664|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] }} [[sn:Tsunaga Shrine]] [[ja:津長神社]] [[sn:sunaga Shrine]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]
